outlook.headline: Installed-base services are broadening as upgrade demand stays firm
outlook.summary: The mix is turning more constructive into 2026Q1: most reads point to growing installed bases, higher attach, and recurring spares/upgrade/service pull, with ASML also benefiting from tight supply as customers use the base to add capacity quickly. Applied Materials, Lam Research, NVIDIA, Ambarella, and Seagate all look like beneficiaries; Skyworks is the outlier with only a descriptive read and no clear service signal. The 0.3x latest mention rate, up 6% versus the trailing average, reinforces that momentum is modestly building rather than fading.

drivers.label: Installed-base expansion supports recurring pull
drivers.detail: A larger base is generating more service, spares, and upgrade activity, which directly lifts recurring revenue.

drivers.label: Higher attach and upgrade intensity
drivers.detail: Service attach and upgrade opportunities are rising, especially where customers are extending the life or capability of existing systems.

drivers.label: Tight supply accelerates capacity additions
drivers.detail: ASML specifically noted tight supply, which supports faster use of the installed base to add capacity and sustain service demand.

drivers.label: Broad mention momentum is edging higher
drivers.detail: Mentions moved from 0.2x to 0.3x recently, suggesting the theme is gaining some traction into the next quarter.

risks: Customer capex pauses could slow installed-base utilization and service pull.
Ambarella’s upgrade opportunity depends on low AI penetration converting into actual upgrades, not just a large base.
Skyworks remains a weaker read because the commentary was descriptive only, limiting conviction on service upside.
